result.preview:
"Postgres query exectution error (ProgrammingError): (psycopg2.errors.UndefinedColumn) column "ownerid" does not exist
LINE 1: SELECT ownerid AS agent_id, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count
               ^

[SQL: SELECT ownerid AS agent_id, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count
FROM casehistory__c
WHERE field__c = 'OwnerId'
  AND createddate >= '2022-04-10' AND createddate < '2023-04-11'
GROUP BY ownerid;]
(Background on this error at: https://sqlalche.me/e/20/f405)"

args.query:
SELECT ownerid AS agent_id, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count
FROM casehistory__c
WHERE field__c = 'OwnerId'
  AND createddate >= '2022-04-10' AND createddate < '2023-04-11'
GROUP BY ownerid;

args.query:
SELECT oldvalue__c AS from_agent, newvalue__c AS to_agent, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count
FROM casehistory__c
WHERE field__c = 'OwnerId'
  AND createddate >= '2022-04-10' AND createddate < '2023-04-11'
GROUP BY oldvalue__c, newvalue__c;

exec_args.sql:
SELECT oldvalue__c AS from_agent, newvalue__c AS to_agent, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count
FROM casehistory__c
WHERE field__c = 'OwnerId'
  AND createddate >= '2022-04-10' AND createddate < '2023-04-11'
GROUP BY oldvalue__c, newvalue__c;

result.preview: "No agent had any recorded case owner transfers in the last 4 quarters (2022-04-10 to 2023-04-10), so every agent who handled more than 0 cases has a transfer count of 0. Because the transfer counts are all tied at 0 and there is no additional tie-breaker specified, there is no single unique agent Id that can be returned as the one with the fewest transfer counts."
